1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: The Reality (i.e. the Day of Resurrection)!

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: How tremendous is the true event!

2.author: Maududi
2.text: And what is that indubitable event?

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: and what is the Resurrection Verifier?

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: What do you comprehend by the concrete reality?

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And what have you understood, how tremendous the true event is!

3.author: Arberry
3.text: And what will teach thee what is the Indubitable?

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And what shall make thee know that which the Inevitable Calamity is.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And what will make you know what the Reality is?

3.author: Itani
3.text: What will make you understand what the Reality is?

3.author: Maududi
3.text: And what do you know what that indubitable event is?

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And what will make you know what Al-Haqqah is

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the reality is!

3.author: Qarai
3.text: What will show you what is the Besieger?!

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: What makes you to know what the Resurrection Verifier is?

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And what can make you know what is the Inevitable Reality?

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: Would that you knew (in detail) what the Inevitable is!

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And what would make you realize what the sure calamity is!

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: And what will make you realize what the Inevitable Hour is?

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And what will make thee realise what the Sure Reality is?

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: The Thamud and 'Ad denied the consequential calamity.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: The tribes of Thamud and A’ad denied the event of great dismay. (The Day of Resurrection)

4.author: Arberry
4.text: Thamood and Ad cried lies to the Clatterer.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: The tribes of Thamud and 'Aad belied the Striking Day.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Thamud and 'Ad people denied the Qari'ah [the striking Hour (of Judgement)]!

4.author: Itani
4.text: Thamood and Aad denied the Catastrophe.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: The Thamud and the Ad denied the (possibility of a) sudden calamity, calling it false.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: (The tribes of) Thamud and A'ad disbelieved in the judgment to come.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: Thamood and Aad belied the Clatterer.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Thamud and 'Aad denied the Striking Calamity.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: The people of Thamud and Ad denied the Day of Judgment.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Samood and Ad called the striking calamity a lie.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: The tribes of Thamud and 'Ad denied that disaster would strike them:

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: The Thamud and the 'Ad People (branded) as false the Stunning Calamity!

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: So destroyed were the Thamud by a storm of thunder and lightning;

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: So regarding the Thamud, they were destroyed by a terrible scream.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: As for Thamood, they were destroyed by the Screamer;

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the outburst.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the awful cry!

5.author: Itani
5.text: As for Thamood, they were annihilated by the Overwhelming.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: Then the Thamud were destroyed by an awesome upheaval;

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the Taghiyah!

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the lightning.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the Cry.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Thamood, they were destroyed by the violent shout (of Gabriel),

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: So as for Thamud, they were destroyed by the overpowering [blast].

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: The Thamuds were destroyed by a violent blast of sound.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Then as to Samood, they were destroyed by an excessively severe punishment.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: the Thamud were destroyed by a terrible storm of thunder and lightning;

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: But the Thamud,- they were destroyed by a terrible Storm of thunder and lightning!

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: And the 'Ad were destroyed by the furious cold blast of roaring wind

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And as for A’ad, they were destroyed by a severe thundering windstorm.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: and as for Ad, they were destroyed by a wind clamorous,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And as for 'Aad they were destroyed by a wind, furious. roaring.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And as for 'Ad, they were destroyed by a furious violent wind;

6.author: Itani
6.text: And as for Aad; they were annihilated by a furious, roaring wind.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and the Ad were destroyed by a furiously raging wind-storm

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And as for `Ad, they were destroyed by a wind, Sarsar `Atiyah!

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And as for A'ad, they were destroyed by a fierce roaring wind,

6.author: Qarai
6.text: And as for ‘Ad, they were destroyed by a fierce icy gale,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: as for Aad, they were destroyed by a howling, violent wind

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And as for 'Aad, they were destroyed by a screaming, violent wind

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: The Ads were destroyed by a swift, destructive gale

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And as to Ad, they were destroyed by a roaring, violent blast.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: and the 'Ad were destroyed by a furious wind

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: And the 'Ad, they were destroyed by a furious Wind, exceedingly violent;

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Waamma AAadun faohlikoo bireehin sarsarin AAatiyatin

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Which He sent to assail them for seven nights and eight days running. You should have seen the people prostrate like the decayed trunks of date-palm trees.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: He forced it upon them with strength, consecutively for seven nights and eight days – so you would see those people overthrown in it, like trunks of date palms fallen down.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: violent that He compelled against them seven nights and eight days, uninterruptedly, and thou mightest see the people laid prostrate in it as if they were the stumps of fallen down palm-trees.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: To which He subjected them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so that thou mightest have seen men during it lying prostrate, as though they were stumps of palms ruined.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: Which Allah imposed on them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so that you could see men lying overthrown (destroyed), as if they were hollow trunks of date-palms!

7.author: Itani
7.text: He unleashed it upon them for seven nights and eight days, in succession. You could see the people tossed around, as though they were stumps of hollow palm-trees.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: which He let loose upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession; so that (if you had been there) you might have seen people lying prostrate, as though they were uprooted trunks of hollowed palm trees.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: Which Allah imposed on them for seven nights and eight days Husum, so that you could see men lying toppled, as if they were trunks of date palms, Khawiyah!

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: Which He imposed on them for seven long nights and eight long days so that thou mightest have seen men lying overthrown, as they were hollow trunks of palm-trees.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: which He clamped on them for seven gruelling nights and eight days, so that you could see the people there lying about prostrate as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: that He subjected upon them for seven nights and eight days consecutively and you might have seen them struck down as if they were the stumps of palm trees that had fallen down.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: Which Allah imposed upon them for seven nights and eight days in succession, so you would see the people therein fallen as if they were hollow trunks of palm trees.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: which continued to strike them for seven and eight days so that eventually you could see the people lying dead like the hollow trunks of uprooted palm-trees.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Which He made to prevail against them for seven nights and eight days unremittingly, so that you might have seen the people therein prostrate as if they were the trunks of hollow palms.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: which God let loose against them for seven nights and eight days unremittingly, so that you could have seen its people lying prostrate as though they were the hollow trunks of palm-trees which had fallen down.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: He made it rage against them seven nights and eight days in succession: so that thou couldst see the (whole) people lying prostrate in its (path), as they had been roots of hollow palm-trees tumbled down!

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Sakhkharaha AAalayhim sabAAa layalin wathamaniyata ayyamin husooman fatara alqawma feeha sarAAa kaannahum aAAjazu nakhlin khawiyatun

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Do you see any trace of them?

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: So do you see any survivor among them?

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Now dost thou see any remnant of them?

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Beholdest thou any of them remaining?

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Do you see any remnants of them?

8.author: Itani
8.text: Can you see any remnant of them?

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Do you now see any trace of them?

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Do you see any remnants of them

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Canst thou (O Muhammad) see any remnant of them?

8.author: Qarai
8.text: So do you see any remaining trace of them?

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Can you see any remnant of them now?

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Then do you see of them any remains?

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Can you see any of their survivors?

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Do you then see of them one remaining?

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Do you see any vestige left of them now?

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Then seest thou any of them left surviving?

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Then came the Pharaoh, and those before him whose habitations were overthrown while they were committing crimes.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And Firaun, and those before him, and the dwellings that were inverted and thrown, had brought error.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Pharaoh likewise, and those before him, and the Subverted Cities -- they committed error,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And Fir'awn and those before him and the overturned cities committed sin.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And Fir'aun (Pharaoh), and those before him, and the cities overthrown [the towns of the people of [Lout (Lot)] committed sin,

9.author: Itani
9.text: Then Pharaoh came, and those before him, and the Overturned Cities steeped in sin.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Pharaoh and those before him and the people of the overturned habitations all engaged in the same great sin.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And Fir`awn, and those before him, and the cities overthrown committed Al-Khati'ah.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And Pharaoh and those before him, and the communities that were destroyed, brought error,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Then Pharaoh and those who were before him, and the towns that were overturned, brought about iniquity.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: Similarly, Pharaoh, and those before him, and the ruined villages, sinned

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And there came Pharaoh and those before him and the overturned cities with sin.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: The Pharaoh, those who lived before him and the people of the Subverted Cities all persisted in doing evil.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And Firon and those before him and the overthrown cities continuously committed sins.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Pharoah and those before him and the inhabitants of the overthrown cities persistently committed grave sins.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And Pharaoh, and those before him, and the Cities Overthrown, committed habitual Sin.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Wajaa firAAawnu waman qablahu waalmutafikatu bialkhatiati

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: When they disobeyed the apostle of their Lord He seized them with an overwhelming punishment.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: They therefore disobeyed the Noble Messengers of their Lord – so He seized them with an intense seizure.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: and they rebelled against the Messenger of their Lord, and He seized them with a surpassing grip.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And they disobeyed their Lord's apostle, so He laid hold of them with a grip increasing.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And they disobeyed their Lord's Messenger, so He punished them with a strong punishment.

10.author: Itani
10.text: But they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with an overpowering grip.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: They did not follow the Messenger of their Lord, and so He seized them with a severe grip.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And they disobeyed their Lord's Messenger, so He seized them with a punishment that was Rabiyah.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, therefor did He grip them with a tightening grip.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: They disobeyed the apostle of their Lord, so He seized them with a terrible seizing.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and rebelled against their Lord's Messenger. So He took them with a stern taking.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a seizure exceeding [in severity].

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: They disobeyed the Messenger of their Lord and He seized them with torment which increased with time.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And they disobeyed the Apostle of their Lord, so He punished them with a vehement punishment.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: They defied their Lord's messenger, so He seized them with an ever-tightening grip.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And disobeyed (each) the messenger of their Lord; so He punished them with an abundant Penalty.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: FaAAasaw rasoola rabbihim faakhathahum akhthatan rabiyatan

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: When the water rose in flood, We bore you in the ark,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: Indeed when the water swelled up, We boarded you onto the ship.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Lo, when the waters rose, We bore you in the running ship

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Verily We! when the water rose, We bare you upon the traversing ark.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Verily! When the water rose beyond its limits [Nuh's (Noah) Flood], We carried you (mankind) in the floating [ship that was constructed by Nuh (Noah)].

11.author: Itani
11.text: When the waters overflowed, We carried you in the cruising ship.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Verily when the water rose to great heights, We bore you upon a floating vessel (i.e. the Ark)

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Verily, when the water rose beyond its limits, We carried you in the ship.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Lo! when the waters rose, We carried you upon the ship

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Indeed when the Flood rose high, We carried you in a floating ark,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: And when the waters rose (high) We carried you in the sailing (Ark),

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Indeed, when the water overflowed, We carried your ancestors in the sailing ship

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: When the flood rose high and covered the whole land, We carried you in the Ark.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Surely We bore you up in the ship when the water rose high,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: But We bore you away in the Ark, when the waters rose high,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: We, when the water (of Noah's Flood) overflowed beyond its limits, carried you (mankind), in the floating (Ark),

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Inna lamma tagha almao hamalnakum fee aljariyati

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: In order to make it a warning for you, and that the ear retentive may preserve it.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: In order to make it a remembrance for you, and in order that the ears that store may remember.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: that We might make it a reminder for you and for heeding ears to hold.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: That We might make it Unto you an admonition, and thot it might be retained by the retaining ears.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: That We might make it a remembrance for you, and the keen ear (person) may (hear and) understand it.

12.author: Itani
12.text: To make it a lesson for you—so that retaining ears may retain it.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: so that We might make it an instructive event for you, and retentive ears might preserve its memory.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: That We might make it an admonition for you and that it might be retained by the retaining ears.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: That We might make it a memorial for you, and that remembering ears (that heard the story) might remember.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: that We might make it a reminder for you, and that receptive ears might remember it.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: making it a Reminder for you, for all attentive ears to retain.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: That We might make it for you a reminder and [that] a conscious ear would be conscious of it.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: as a lesson for you, but only attentive ears will retain it.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: So that We may make it a reminder to you, and that the retaining ear might retain it.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: so that We might make it a reminder for you and so that attentive ears might retain it.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: That We might make it a Message unto you, and that ears (that should hear the tale and) retain its memory should bear its (lessons) in remembrance.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: LinajAAalaha lakum tathkiratan wataAAiyaha othunun waAAiyatun

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: When the single blast is sounded on the trumpet,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: So when the Trumpet will be blown, with a sudden single blow.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: So, when the Trumpet is blown with a single blast

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And when the Trumpet shall sound a single blast.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Then when the Trumpet will be blown with one blowing (the first one),

13.author: Itani
13.text: Then, when the Trumpet is sounded a single time.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: So when the Trumpet is blown with a single blast

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Then when the Trumpet will be blown with one blowing.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And when the trumpet shall sound one blast

13.author: Qarai
13.text: When the Trumpet is blown with a single blast

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: When a single blow is blown on the Horn,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Then when the Horn is blown with one blast

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: With the first blast of sound from the trumpet,

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And when the trumpet is blown with a single blast,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: When a single blast is blown on the trumpet,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Then, when one blast is sounded on the Trumpet,

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Faitha nufikha fee alssoori nafkhatun wahidatun

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And the earth and mountains heaved and crushed to powder with one levelling blow,

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And the earth and the mountains will be lifted up and crushed with a single crush.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and crushed with a single blow,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And the earth and the mountains shall be borne and the twain shall be crushed with a single crash.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And the earth and the mountains shall be removed from their places, and crushed with a single crushing,

14.author: Itani
14.text: And the earth and the mountains are lifted up, and crushed, with a single crush.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and the earth and the mountains are carried aloft and are crushed to bits at one stroke,

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And the earth and the mountains shall be removed from their places, and crushed with a single crushing.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And the earth with the mountains shall be lifted up and crushed with one crash,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and the earth and the mountains are lifted and levelled with a single levelling,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: when the earth with all its mountains is lifted up and crushed with a single blow,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And the earth and the mountains are lifted and leveled with one blow -

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: the earth and mountains will be raised up high and crushed all together.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And the earth and the mountains are borne away and crushed with a single crushing.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and then crushed with a single blow,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And the earth is moved, and its mountains, and they are crushed to powder at one stroke,-

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Wahumilati alardu waaljibalu fadukkata dakkatan wahidatan

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: On that Day will come what is to come.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: So that is the day when the forthcoming event will occur.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: then, on that day, the Terror shall come to pass,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Then on that Day shall happen the Event.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Then on that Day shall the (Great) Event befall,

15.author: Itani
15.text: On that Day, the Event will come to pass.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: on that Day shall that indubitable event come to pass;

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Then on that Day shall the Event occur.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Then, on that day will the Event befall.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: then, on that day, will the Imminent [Hour] befall

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: on that Day, the Event occurs.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Then on that Day, the Resurrection will occur,

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: On that day, the inevitable event will take place

15.author: Shakir
15.text: On that day shall the great event come to pass,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: on that Day the Great Event will come to pass.

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: On that Day shall the (Great) Event come to pass.

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: The sky will cleave asunder on that day and fall to pieces.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: And the heaven will split asunder – so on that day it will be unstable.

16.author: Arberry
16.text: and heaven shall be split, for upon that day it shall be very frail,

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: And the heaven shall be rent in sunder, it on that Day shall be frail.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: And the heaven will split asunder, for that Day it (the heaven will be frail (weak), and torn up,

16.author: Itani
16.text: And the heaven will crack; so on that Day it will be frail.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: when the sky will be rent asunder, the grip holding it together having loosened on that Day,

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: And the heaven will be rent asunder, for that Day it will be frail and torn up.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: And the heaven will split asunder, for that day it will be frail.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: and the sky will be split open—for it will be frail on that day—

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: The heaven will be split; because on that Day it will be frail.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: And the heaven will split [open], for that Day it is infirm.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: and the heavens will be rent asunder,

16.author: Shakir
16.text: And the heaven shall cleave asunder, so that on that day it shall be frail,

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: And the sky will be rent asunder, for on that Day it will be so frail.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: And the sky will be rent asunder, for it will that Day be flimsy,

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: On its fringes will be angels, eight of them, bearing their Lord's throne aloft.

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And the angels will be on its sides; and on that day, eight angels will carry the Throne of your Lord above them.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and the angels shall stand upon its borders, and upon that day eight shall carry above them the Throne of thy Lord.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And the angels shall be on the borders thereof; and on that Day eight shall bear over them the Throne of thy Lord.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And the angels will be on its sides, and eight angels will, that Day, bear the Throne of your Lord above them.

17.author: Itani
17.text: And the angels will be ranged around its borders, while eight will be carrying the Throne of your Lord above them that Day.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: and the angels will stand on the sides, with eight of them bearing aloft the Throne of your Lord on that Day.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And the angels will be on its sides, and eight angels will, that Day, bear the Throne of your Lord above them.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And the angels will be on the sides thereof, and eight will uphold the Throne of thy Lord that day, above them.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: with the angels all over it, and the Throne of your Lord will be borne that day by eight [angels].

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: The angels will stand on all its sides. And on that Day, eight (of them) will carry the Throne of your Lord above them.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And the angels are at its edges. And there will bear the Throne of your Lord above them, that Day, eight [of them].

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: and will turn frail, losing all force. The angels will be around the heavens and on that day eight of them will carry the Throne of your Lord above all the creatures.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And the angels shall be on the sides thereof; and above them eight shall bear on that day your Lord's power.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: The angels will appear by its sides and, on that Day, eight [angels] will bear your Lord's throne above them.

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: And the angels will be on its sides, and eight will, that Day, bear the Throne of thy Lord above them.

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Waalmalaku AAala arjaiha wayahmilu AAarsha rabbika fawqahum yawmaithin thamaniyatun

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: You will then be set before Him, and not one of you will remain unexposed.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: On that day all of you will be brought forth, so none among you wishing to hide will be able to hide.

18.author: Arberry
18.text: On that day you shall be exposed, not one secret of yours concealed.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: The Day whereon ye shall be mustered nothing hidden by you shall be hidden.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: That Day shall you be brought to Judgement, not a secret of you will be hidden.

18.author: Itani
18.text: On that Day you will be exposed, and no secret of yours will remain hidden.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: That will be the Day when you shall be brought forth (before Allah) and no secret of yours shall remain hidden.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: That Day shall you be brought to Judgement, not a secret of you will be hidden.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: On that day ye will be exposed; not a secret of you will be hidden.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: That day you will be presented [before your Lord]: none of your secrets will remain hidden.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: On that Day you shall be exposed, and no secret of yours will remain hidden.

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: That Day, you will be exhibited [for judgement]; not hidden among you is anything concealed.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: On that day all your secrets will be exposed.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: On that day you shall be exposed to view-- no secret of yours shall remain hidden.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: On that Day you will be brought to judgement and none of your secrets will remain hidden.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: That Day shall ye be brought to Judgment: not an act of yours that ye hide will be hidden.

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Yawmaithin tuAAradoona la takhfa minkum khafiyatun

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: He who is given his ledger in his right hand, will say: "Here, read my ledger.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: So whoever is given his book in his right hand – he will say, “Take, read my account!”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: Then as for him who is given his book in his right hand, he shall say, 'Here, take and read my book!

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Then as to him who will be vouchsafed his book in his right hand, he shall say: here! read my book!

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Then as for him who will be given his Record in his right hand will say: "Take, read my Record!

19.author: Itani
19.text: As for him who is given his book in his right hand, he will say, “Here, take my book and read it.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: On that Day, he whose Record is given to him in his right hand will say: “Lo! Read my Record!

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Then as for him who will be given his Record in his right hand will say: "Here! read my Record!"

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Then, as for him who is given his record in his right hand, he will say: Take, read my book!

19.author: Qarai
19.text: As for him who is given his book in his right hand, he will say, ‘Here, take and read my book!

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Then, he who is given his book in his right hand will say: 'Here, take and read my book!

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: So as for he who is given his record in his right hand, he will say, "Here, read my record!

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: Those who will receive the books of the records of their deeds in their right hands will say, "Come and read my record.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Then as for him who is given his book in his right hand, he will say: Lo! read my book:

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Then he who is given his record in his right hand will exclaim, "Here is my record, read it.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: Then he that will be given his Record in his right hand will say: "Ah here! Read ye my Record!

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Faamma man ootiya kitabahu biyameenihi fayaqoolu haomu iqraoo kitabiyah

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: I was certain I'll be given my account."

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: “I was certain that I will confront my account.”

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Certainly I thought that I should encounter my reckoning.'

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Verily I was sure that I should be a meeter of my reckoning.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: "Surely, I did believe that I shall meet my Account!"

20.author: Itani
20.text: I knew I would be held accountable.”

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Verily I was sure that I would be handed over my account.”

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: "Surely, I did believe that I shall meet my account!"

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Surely I knew that I should have to meet my reckoning.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Indeed I knew that I will encounter my account [of deeds].’

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Indeed, I knew that I should come to my reckoning'

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Indeed, I was certain that I would be meeting my account."

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: I was sure that the record of my deeds would be shown to me".

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Surely I knew that I shall meet my account.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Surely, I knew that I should meet my reckoning,"

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: "I did really understand that my Account would (One Day) reach me!"

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: So he shall have an agreeable life

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: He is therefore in the desired serenity.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: So he shall be in a pleasing life

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Then he shall be in a life well-pleasing -

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: So he shall be in a life, well-pleasing.

21.author: Itani
21.text: So he will be in pleasant living.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Then he shall find himself in a life of bliss;

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: So, he shall be in a life, well-pleasing.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Then he will be in blissful state

21.author: Qarai
21.text: So he will have a pleasant life,

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: His shall be a pleasing life

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: So he will be in a pleasant life -

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: They will have a pleasant life

21.author: Shakir
21.text: So he shall be in a life of pleasure,

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: so he will live in a state of Bliss

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And he will be in a life of Bliss,

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: With fruits hanging low within reach,

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: The fruit clusters of which are hanging down.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Clusters whereof shall be near at hand.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: The fruits in bunches whereof will be low and near at hand.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: the clusters of whose fruit will be hanging low to be within reach (of the inmates of Paradise).

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: The fruits in bunches whereof will be low and near at hand.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Whereof the clusters are in easy reach.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: whose clusters [of fruits] will be within easy reach.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Its [fruit] to be picked hanging near.

23.author: Shakir
23.text: The fruits of which are near at hand:

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: with clusters of fruit within easy reach.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: The Fruits whereof (will hang in bunches) low and near.

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: (And told:) "Eat and drink to your fill as reward for (good) deeds you had done in days of yore."

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: “Eat and drink with pleasure – the reward of what you sent ahead, in the past days.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: 'Eat and drink with wholesome appetite for that you did long ago, in the days gone by.'

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Eat and drink with benefit for that which ye sent on beforehand in dayspast.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Eat and drink at ease for that which you have sent on before you in days past!

24.author: Itani
24.text: “Eat and drink merrily for what you did in the days gone by.”

24.author: Maududi
24.text: (They will be told): “Eat and drink with good cheer as a reward for the good deeds you did in the days that have passed by.”

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Eat and drink at ease for that which you have sent on before you in days past!

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: (And it will be said unto those therein): Eat and drink at ease for that which ye sent on before you in past days.

24.author: Qarai
24.text: [He will be told]: ‘Enjoy your food and drink, for what you had sent in advance in past days [for your future life].’

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: (It will be said): 'Eat and drink with a good appetite because of what you did in days long passed'

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: [They will be told], "Eat and drink in satisfaction for what you put forth in the days past."

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: Such people will be told, "Eat and drink with pleasure as the reward for what you did in the past".

24.author: Shakir
24.text: Eat and drink pleasantly for what you did beforehand in the days gone by.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: We shall say to him, "Eat and drink joyfully as a reward for the good deeds you did in days gone by."

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: "Eat ye and drink ye, with full satisfaction; because of the (good) that ye sent before you, in the days that are gone!"

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima aslaftum fee alayyami alkhaliyati

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: But whosoever gets his ledger in his left hand, will say: "Would that I were never given my ledger,

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And whoever is given his book in his left hand – he will say, “Alas, if only my account were not given to me!”

25.author: Arberry
25.text: But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, he shall say, 'Would that I had not been given my book

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Then as to him who shall be vouchsafed his book in his left hand, he shall say: Oh! would that I had not been vouchsafed my book.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: But as for him who will be given his Record in his left hand, will say: "I wish that I had not been given my Record!

25.author: Itani
25.text: But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, he will say, “I wish I was never given my book.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: As for him whose Record will be given to him in his left hand, he will exclaim: “Would that I had never been given my Record,

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: But as for him who will be given his Record in his left hand, will say: "I wish that I had not been given my Record!"

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: But as for him who is given his record in his left hand, he will say: Oh, would that I had not been given my book

25.author: Qarai
25.text: But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, he will say, ‘I wish I had not been given my book,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: But, he who is given his book in his left hand will say: 'Woe to me, would that my book had not been given to me!

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: But as for he who is given his record in his left hand, he will say, "Oh, I wish I had not been given my record

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: However, those who will receive the books of the records of their deeds in their left hands will say, "We wish that this record had never been given to us

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And as for him who is given his book in his left hand he shall say: O would that my book had never been given me:

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: But he who is given his record in his left hand will say, "If only I had never been given my Record

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: And he that will be given his Record in his left hand, will say: "Ah! Would that my Record had not been given to me!

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Waamma man ootiya kitabahu bishimalihi fayaqoolu ya laytanee lam oota kitabiyah

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: “And had never come to know my account!”

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Nor known whatever was my reckoning!

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: "And that I had never known, how my Account is?

26.author: Itani
26.text: And never knew what my account was.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: and had not known my account.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: "And that I had never known how my account is!"

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And knew not what my reckoning!

26.author: Qarai
26.text: nor had I ever known what my account is!

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: Nor that I knew my reckoning!

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And had not known what is my account.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: and that we would never knew what our records contained.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And I had not known what my account was:

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: and knew nothing of my reckoning.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: "And that I had never realised how my account (stood)!

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: I wish death had put an end to me.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: “Alas, if only it had been just death.”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Would it had been the end!

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: Oh, would that it had been the ending!

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: "I wish, would that it had been my end (death)!

27.author: Itani
27.text: If only it was the end.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: Oh! Would that the death that came to me in the world had made an end of me!

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: "Would that it had been my end!"

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: Oh, would that it had been death!

27.author: Qarai
27.text: I wish death had been the end of it all!

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Would that it (my death) had ended it all!

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: I wish my death had been the decisive one.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Would that death had taken us away for good.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: O would that it had made an end (of me):

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: How I wish my death had ended all.

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: "Ah! Would that (Death) had made an end of me!

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Of no use was even my wealth.

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: “My wealth did not in the least benefit me.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: My wealth has not availed me,

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: My riches have: availed me not;

28.author: Maududi
28.text: My riches have not availed me,

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: My wealth hath not availed me,

28.author: Qarai
28.text: My wealth did not avail me.

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: My wealth has not helped me a thing

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: My wealth has not availed me.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Our wealth is of no benefit to us

28.author: Shakir
28.text: My wealth has availed me nothing:

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: My wealth has been of no use to me.

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: "Of no profit to me has been my wealth!

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: Vanished has my power from me."

29.author: Arberry
29.text: my authority is gone from me.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: There hath perished from me my authority.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: "My power and arguments (to defend myself) have gone from me!"

29.author: Itani
29.text: My power has vanished from me.”

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: My power hath gone from me.

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: and my authority has been destroyed'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: Gone from me is my authority."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: and our belief has destroyed us".

29.author: Shakir
29.text: My authority is gone away from me.

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: It will be said, “Seize him, and shackle him.”

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Lay hold of him and chain him;

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: (It will be said): "Seize him and fetter him,

30.author: Maududi
30.text: (A command will be issued): “Seize him and shackle him,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: (It will be said): "Seize him and fetter him;"

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: (It will be said): Take him and fetter him

30.author: Qarai
30.text: [The angels will be told:] ‘Seize him, and fetter him!

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: (It will be said): 'Take him and bind him.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: [Allah will say], "Seize him and shackle him.

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: The angels will be told, "Seize and chain them,

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Lay hold on him, then put a chain on him,

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: (The stern command will say): "Seize ye him, and bind ye him,

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Then cast him to be burnt in Hell;

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: “Then hurl him into the blazing fire.”

31.author: Arberry
31.text: and then roast him in Hell,

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: Then in the Scorch roast him

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Then throw him in the blazing Fire.

31.author: Itani
31.text: Then scorch him in the Blaze.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: then cast him in the Fire,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: "Then throw him in the blazing Fire."

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: And then expose him to hell-fire

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: then throw them into hell to be heated up therein.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: Then cast him into the burning fire,

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: and then let him enter Hell.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: "And burn ye him in the Blazing Fire.

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And string him to a chain seventy cubits long.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: “Then bind him inside a chain which is seventy arm-lengths.”

32.author: Arberry
32.text: then in a chain of seventy cubits' length insert him!

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: Then, in a chain whereof the length is seventy Cubits, bind him.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: "Then fasten him with a chain whereof the length is seventy cubits!"

32.author: Itani
32.text: Then in a chain which length is seventy cubits tie him up.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: then fasten him with a chain, seventy cubits long.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: "Then fasten him on a chain whereof the length is seventy cubits!"

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And then insert him in a chain whereof the length is seventy cubits.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: Then bind him in a chain, seventy cubits in length.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: then in a chain seventy arm's lengths long insert him.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: Then into a chain whose length is seventy cubits insert him."

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: Fasten a chain to them - seventy cubits long -

32.author: Shakir
32.text: Then thrust him into a chain the length of which is seventy cubits.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Then fasten him with a chain seventy cubits long:

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: "Further, make him march in a chain, whereof the length is seventy cubits!

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Thumma fee silsilatin tharAAuha sabAAoona thiraAAan faoslukoohu

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: He did not believe in God the supreme,

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: “Indeed he refused to accept faith in Allah, the Greatest.”

33.author: Arberry
33.text: Behold, he never believed in God the All-mighty,

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: Verily he was wont not to believe in Allah, the Mighty.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: Verily, He used not to believe in Allah, the Most Great,

33.author: Itani
33.text: For he would not believe in God the Great.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: He would not believe in Allah, the Most Great;

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: Verily, he used not to believe in Allah, the Most Great,

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: Lo! He used not to believe in Allah the Tremendous,

33.author: Qarai
33.text: Indeed he had no faith in Allah, the All-supreme,

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: He did not believe in Allah, the Great,

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: Indeed, he did not used to believe in Allah, the Most Great,

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: they did not believe in the great God,

33.author: Shakir
33.text: Surely he did not believe in Allah, the Great,

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: for he did not believe in Almighty God,

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: "This was he that would not believe in Allah Most High.

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Innahu kana la yuminu biAllahi alAAatheemi

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: Nor urged others to feed the poor.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: “And did not urge to feed the needy.”

34.author: Arberry
34.text: and he never urged the feeding of the needy;

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: Nor he urged on others the feeding of the poor.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And urged not on the feeding of Al-Miskin (the poor),

34.author: Itani
34.text: Nor would he advocate the feeding of the destitute.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: nor would he urge the feeding of the poor.

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And urged not the feeding of the poor.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And urged not on the feeding of the wretched.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: and he did not urge the feeding of the needy,

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: nor did he urge the feeding of the needy.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: Nor did he encourage the feeding of the poor.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: nor were they concerned with feeding the destitute.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: Nor did he urge the feeding of the poor.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: nor did he feel any urge to feed the needy,

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: "And would not encourage the feeding of the indigent!

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: That is why he has no friend today,

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: “So he does not have any friend here this day.”

35.author: Arberry
35.text: therefore he today has not here one loyal friend,

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Wherefore for him here this Day there is no friend.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: So no friend has he here this Day,

35.author: Itani
35.text: So he has no friend here today.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: Today he has been left here friendless;

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: So, no friend has he here this Day,

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Therefor hath he no lover here this day,

35.author: Qarai
35.text: so he has no friend here today,

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Today he shall have no loyal friend here,

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: So there is not for him here this Day any devoted friend

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: On this day, they will have no friends

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Therefore he has not here today a true friend,

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: so today he has no friend here,

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: "So no friend hath he here this Day.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: “Nor any food except the pus discharged from the people of hell.”

36.author: Arberry
36.text: neither any food saving foul pus,

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: Nor any food save filthy corruption.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: Nor any food except filth from the washing of wounds,

36.author: Maududi
36.text: and has no food except the filth from the washing of wounds,

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: Nor any food except filth from Ghislin.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: nor any food except foul pus

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: Nor any food except from the discharge of wounds;

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: and the only food he has is filth

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: "Nor hath he any food except the corruption from the washing of wounds,

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Which none but the hellish eat."

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: “Which none except the guilty shall eat.”

37.author: Arberry
37.text: that none excepting the sinners eat.'

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: None shall eat it but the sinners.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: None will eat except the Khati'un (sinners, disbelievers, polytheists, etc.).

37.author: Maududi
37.text: which only the sinners will eat.”

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: None will eat it except the Khati'un.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: which no one shall eat except the iniquitous.’

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: None will eat it except the sinners.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: Which none but the wrongdoers eat.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: which no one will eat except the sinners.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: "Which none do eat but those in sin."

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: So, I call to witness what you see

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: So by oath of the things you see.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: No! I swear by that you see

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: I swear by that which ye see.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: So I swear by whatsoever you see,

38.author: Itani
38.text: Indeed, I swear by what you see.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: But no; I swear by what you see,

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: So, I swear by whatsoever you see,

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: But nay! I swear by all that ye see

38.author: Qarai
38.text: I swear by what you see

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: I swear by all that you can see,

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: So I swear by what you see

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: I do not need to swear by what you see

38.author: Shakir
38.text: But nay! I swear by that which you see,

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: But nay, I swear by all that you can see

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: So I do call to witness what ye see,

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: And what you do not see,

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And by oath of those you do not see.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: and by that you do not see,

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And that which ye see not.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And by whatsoever you see not,

39.author: Itani
39.text: And by what you do not see.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: and by what you do not see,

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And by whatsoever you see not,

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: And all that ye see not

39.author: Qarai
39.text: and what you do not see:

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: and all that you do not see,

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And what you do not see

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: and what you do not see

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And that which you do not see.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: as well as all that you cannot see:

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: That this is indeed the word of the noble Messenger,

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: This Qur’an is the speech of Allah with a gracious Noble Messenger.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: it is the speech of a noble Messenger.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: That it is surely the speech brought by messenger honourable.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: That this is verily the word of an honoured Messenger [i.e. Jibrael (Gabriel) or Muhammad SAW which he has brought from Allah].

40.author: Itani
40.text: It is the speech of a noble messenger.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: that this is the speech of an honourable Messenger,

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: That this is verily the word of an honored Messenger.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: That it is indeed the speech of an illustrious messenger.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: it is indeed the speech of a noble apostle

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: that this is the speech of a noble Messenger.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: [That] indeed, the Qur'an is the word of a noble Messenger.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: because the Quran is certainly the word of a reverent messenger.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: Most surely, it is the Word brought by an honored Apostle,

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: most surely, this is the word brought by a noble messenger,

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: That this is verily the word of an honoured messenger;

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: And not the word of a poet. How little is it that you believe!

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And it is not the speech of a poet; how little do you believe!

41.author: Arberry
41.text: It is not the speech of a poet (little do you believe)

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And it is not the speech of a poet. Little it is that ye believe

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: It is not the word of a poet, little is that you believe!

41.author: Itani
41.text: And it is not the speech of a poet—little do you believe.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: not the speech of a poet. Little do you believe!

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: It is not the word of a poet, little is that you believe!

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: It is not poet's speech - little is it that ye believe!

41.author: Qarai
41.text: and it is not the speech of a poet. Little is the faith that you have!

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: It is not the speech of a poet little do you believe

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And it is not the word of a poet; little do you believe.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: It is not the word of a poet but only a few of you have faith,

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And it is not the word of a poet; little is it that you believe;

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: it is not the word of a poet -- how little you believe! --

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: It is not the word of a poet: little it is ye believe!

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Wama huwa biqawli shaAAirin qaleelan ma tuminoona

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer. Little is it that you reflect!

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Nor is it the speech of a soothsayer; how little do you ponder!

42.author: Arberry
42.text: nor the speech of a soothsayer (little do you remember).

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Nor is it the speech of a sooth- sayer. Little are ye admonished.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer (or a foreteller), little is that you remember!

42.author: Itani
42.text: Nor is it the speech of a soothsayer—little do you take heed.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: Nor is this the speech of a soothsayer. Little do you reflect!

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer, little is that you remember!

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: Nor diviner's speech - little is it that ye remember!

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Nor is it the speech of a soothsayer. Little is the admonition that you take!

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: nor is it the speech of a soothsayer little do you remember.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little do you remember.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: nor is it the work of a soothsayer but only a few of you take heed.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little is it that you mind.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer -- how little you reflect!

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: Nor is it the word of a soothsayer: little admonition it is ye receive.

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Wala biqawli kahinin qaleelan ma tathakkaroona

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: It has been sent down by the Lord of all the worlds.

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Sent down by the Lord Of The Creation.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: A sending down from the Lord of all Being.

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: It is a Revelation from the Lord of the worlds.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: This is the Revelation sent down from the Lord of the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists).

43.author: Itani
43.text: It is a revelation from the Lord of the Worlds.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: It has been revealed by the Lord of the Universe.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: This is the revelation sent down from the Lord of all that exists.

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: It is a revelation from the Lord of the Worlds.

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Gradually sent down from the Lord of all the worlds.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: (It is) a sending down from the Lord of all the Worlds.

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: [It is] a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: It is a revelation from the Lord of the Universe.

43.author: Shakir
43.text: It is a revelation from the Lord of the worlds.

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: It is a revelation sent down by the Sustainer of the Universe:

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: (This is) a Message sent down from the Lord of the Worlds.

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Had he attributed falsely any words to Us,

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: And had he fabricated just one matter upon Us –

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Had he invented against Us any sayings,

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: And if he had forged concerning us some discourses.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: And if he (Muhammad SAW) had forged a false saying concerning Us (Allah),

44.author: Itani
44.text: Had he falsely attributed some statements to Us.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: And if he [i.e., the Prophets] had forged this Discourse and thereafter ascribed it to Us,

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: And if he had forged a false saying concerning Us,

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: And if he had invented false sayings concerning Us,

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Had he faked any sayings in Our name,

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Had he invented sayings against Us,

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: And if Muhammad had made up about Us some [false] sayings,

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Had Muhammad invented some words against Us,.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: And if he had fabricated against Us some of the sayings,

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: if he had invented any lies about Us,

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: And if the messenger were to invent any sayings in Our name,

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: We would have seized him by his right hand,

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: We would have definitely taken revenge from him.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: We would have seized him by the right hand,

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: We surely had lain hold of him by the right hand.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: We surely should have seized him by his right hand (or with power and might),

45.author: Itani
45.text: We would have seized him by the right arm.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: We would surely have seized him by the right hand,

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: We surely would have seized him by his right hand,

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: We assuredly had taken him by the right hand

45.author: Qarai
45.text: We would have surely seized him by the right hand

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: We would have seized him by the right hand

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: We would have seized him by the right hand;

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: We would have caught hold of him by his right hand

45.author: Shakir
45.text: We would certainly have seized him by the right hand,

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: We would indeed have seized him by his right hand

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: We should certainly seize him by his right hand,

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: Then would have cut off his heart’s artery.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: then We would surely have cut his life-vein

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: And then certainly should have cut off his life artery (Aorta),

46.author: Maududi
46.text: and then severed his life vein;

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: And then We certainly would have cut off Al-Watin from him,

46.author: Qarai
46.text: and then cut off his aorta,

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: then, We would surely have cut from him the aorta (vein)

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: Then We would have cut from him the aorta.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Then We would certainly have cut off his aorta.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: and would indeed have cut his life-vein,

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: And We should certainly then cut off the artery of his heart:

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: And not one of you would have been able to stop (Us).

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: Then none among you would be his saviour.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: and not one of you could have defended him.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And not one of you would have withheld us from punishing him.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And none of you could withhold Us from (punishing) him.

47.author: Itani
47.text: And none of you could have restrained Us from him.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: and not one of you would have been able to withhold Us from doing so.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And none of you could have prevented it from him.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And not one of you could have held Us off from him.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: and none of you could have held Us off from him.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: not one of you could have prevented it from him.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And there is no one of you who could prevent [Us] from him.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: None of you would be able to prevent Us from doing this to him.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And not one of you could have withheld Us from him.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: and none of you could have held Us off from him.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: Nor could any of you withhold him (from Our wrath).

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Fama minkum min ahadin AAanhu hajizeena

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: It is really a reminder for those who fear God and follow the straight path.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And indeed this Qur’an is an advice for the pious.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Surely it is a Reminder to the godfearing;

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And verily it is an Admonition Unto the God-fearing.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And verily, this Quran is a Reminder for the Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2).

48.author: Itani
48.text: Surely, it is a message for the righteous.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: Surely it is a Good Counsel for the God-fearing.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And verily, this is a Reminder for those who have Taqwa.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And lo! it is a warrant unto those who ward off (evil).

48.author: Qarai
48.text: Indeed it is a reminder for the Godwary.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: Indeed, it is a Reminder to those who fear Allah,

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And indeed, the Qur'an is a reminder for the righteous.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: The Quran is certainly a reminder for the pious ones.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And most surely it is a reminder for those who guard (against evil).

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: And surely it is an admonition to the God-fearing.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: But verily this is a Message for the Allah-fearing.

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: We certainly know that some among you do deny it.

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: And indeed We know that some among you are deniers.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: but We know that some of you will cry lies.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: And verily We! We know that some among you are beliers thereof.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: And verily, We know that there are some among you that belie (this Quran). [Tafsir At-Tabari, Vol. 29, Page 68]

49.author: Itani
49.text: And We know that some of you will reject it.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: We certainly know that some among you will give the lie to it,

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: And verily, We know that there are some among you that deny.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: And lo! We know that some among you will deny (it).

49.author: Qarai
49.text: Indeed We know that there are some among you who deny [it].

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: We know that there are some among you who will belie.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: And indeed, We know that among you are deniers.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: We certainly know that some of you have rejected it

49.author: Shakir
49.text: And most surely We know that some of you are rejecters.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: We know very well that there are some among you who reject Our signs --

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: And We certainly know that there are amongst you those that reject (it).

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: It is surely the nemesis of unbelievers.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And indeed it is a despair for the disbelievers.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: Surely it is a sorrow to the unbelievers;

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And verily it shall be an occasion of anguish Unto the infidels.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And indeed it (this Quran) will be an anguish for the disbelievers (on the Day of Resurrection).

50.author: Itani
50.text: And it is surely a source of grief for the unbelievers.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: and surely it will be a cause of regret for the unbelievers.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And indeed it will be an anguish for the disbelievers.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And lo! it is indeed an anguish for the disbelievers.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: And indeed it will be a [matter of] regret for the faithless.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: Indeed, it is a sorrow to the unbelievers

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And indeed, it will be [a cause of] regret upon the disbelievers.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: and (on the Day of Judgment) this will be a great source of regret for the unbelievers.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And most surely it is a great grief to the unbelievers.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: it will be a source of bitter regret for those who deny the truth --

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: But truly (Revelation) is a cause of sorrow for the Unbelievers.

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: And He, He is indeed the ultimate Reality.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: And indeed it is a certain Truth.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: yet indeed it is the truth of certainty.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And verily it is the truth of assured certainty.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And Verily, it (this Quran) is an absolute truth with certainty.

51.author: Itani
51.text: Yet it is the absolute truth.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: Certainly it is a Truth of absolute certainty.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And verily, it (this Qur'an) is an absolute truth with certainty.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: And lo! it is absolute truth.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: yet it is a certain truth.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And indeed, it is the truth of certainty.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: This is the Truth beyond any doubt.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And most surely it is the true certainty

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: But verily it is Truth of assured certainty.

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: So glorify your Lord, the most supreme.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: Therefore (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), proclaim the purity of your Lord, the Greatest.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Then magnify the Name of thy Lord, the All-mighty.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Wherefore hallow thou the name of thy Lord, the Mighty.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: So glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

52.author: Itani
52.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Magnificent.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: So glorify the name of your Lord Most Great.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: So, glorify the Name of your Lord, the Most Great.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: So glorify the name of thy Tremendous Lord.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: So celebrate the Name of your Lord, the All-supreme.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Exalt the Name of your Lord, the Great.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: So exalt the name of your Lord, the Most Great.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: (Muhammad), glorify the name of your Lord, the Great One.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: Therefore-glorify the name of your Lord, the Great.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: So glorify the name of your Lord, the Almighty.

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: So glorify the name of thy Lord Most High.
